(born 22 November 1987 in [Colón](/wiki/Col%C3%B3n,_Panama "Colón, Panama")) is a retired [Panamanian](/wiki/Panama "Panama") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") attacking midfielder.

[El panameño Nelson Barahona es nuevo jugador del Medellín](http://www.futbolred.com/liga-postobon/noticias/pretemporada2010/el-panameno-nelson-barahona-es-nuevo-jugador-de-independiente-medellin/6918927)

["Nelson Barahona reforzará al Caracas"](https://web.archive.org/web/20110103191004/http://caracasfutbolclub.com/esite/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=412:nelson-barahona-reforzara-al-caracas&catid=9:1ra-division). Archived from [the original](http://caracasfutbolclub.com/esite/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=412:nelson-barahona-reforzara-al-caracas&catid=9:1ra-division) on 3 January 2011. Retrieved 16 July 2012.

[Barahona se integró al Huila El panameño compartirá vestuario con sus compatriotas Amílcar Henríquez y Alejandro Vélez](http://impresa.prensa.com/deportes/Bara

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Barahona* and the second or maternal family name is  *Collins*.

**Nelson Alberto Barahona Collins** (born 22 November 1987 in [Colón](/wiki/Col%C3%B3n,_Panama "Colón, Panama")) is a retired [Panamanian](/wiki/Panama "Panama") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") attacking midfielder.

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nelson_Barahona&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Nicknamed *el Ruso*, Barahona played numerous of years in [Árabe Unido](/wiki/Club_Deportivo_%C3%81rabe_Unido "Club Deportivo Árabe Unido")'s youth team before in 2007 making his debut with the senior team. After having several good appearances with the club and the [Panama youth team](/wiki/Panama_national_under-20_football_team "Panama national under-20 football team") he moved to [Uruguayan](/wiki/Uruguay "Uruguay") side [Fénix](/wiki/Central_Atletico_F%C3%A9nix "Central Atletico Fénix"). *Russo* made his league debut with [Fénix](/wiki/Central_Atletico_F%C3%A9nix "Central Atletico Fénix") as a second-half substitute on 27 February 2008 against [Nacional](/wiki/Club_Nacional_de_Football "Club Nacional de Football").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Unfortunately, [Fénix](/wiki/Central_Atletico_F%C3%A9nix "Central Atletico Fénix") were relegated and Barahona returned to [ANAPROF](/wiki/ANAPROF "ANAPROF") side [Árabe Unido](/wiki/Club_Deportivo_%C3%81rabe_Unido "Club Deportivo Árabe Unido") where he was crowned champion of the [ANAPROF Clausura 2008](/wiki/ANAPROF_Clausura_2008 "ANAPROF Clausura 2008").

On 28 February 2009 Barahona signed a 1-year loan contract with [Colombian](/wiki/Colombian_people "Colombian people") side [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila") making him the second Panamanian in team after [Amilcar Henriquez](/wiki/Amilcar_Henriquez "Amilcar Henriquez") was signed in December 2008.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He played his last game for [Árabe Unido](/wiki/Club_Deportivo_%C3%81rabe_Unido "Club Deportivo Árabe Unido") on 27 February 2009.

By 2010 he was transferred to [Independiente Medellín](/wiki/Independiente_Medell%C3%ADn "Independiente Medellín"), a club with which he had the chance to play the [2010 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2010_Copa_Libertadores "2010 Copa Libertadores").[[3]](#cite_note-3) At the end of the year, he was loaned to [Venezuelan](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") side [Caracas FC](/wiki/Caracas_FC "Caracas FC").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In mid-2011 he returned to [Independiente Medellín](/wiki/Independiente_Medell%C3%ADn "Independiente Medellín"), club owner of his sports rights, but on 2 September 2011 he was hired by [Deportivo Táchira](/wiki/Deportivo_T%C3%A1chira "Deportivo Táchira") of [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") whom he subsequently left in December 2011.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On 17 January 2012 [Atlético Huila](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Huila "Atlético Huila") confirmed him as the new reinforcement for the 2012 season, Barahona joining compatriots [Amílcar Henríquez](/wiki/Am%C3%ADlcar_Henr%C3%ADquez "Amílcar Henríquez") and [Alejandro Vélez](/wiki/Alejandro_V%C3%A9lez "Alejandro Vélez") at the club.[[6]](#cite_note-6) This is his second stay in the club. In February 2013, he signed with [Itagüí](/wiki/Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD_F.C. "Itagüí F.C.")[[7]](#cite_note-7) and he moved to [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera "Alianza Petrolera") in December 2013.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2015, Barahona returned to Panama after seven years abroad to play for [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco_F.C. "San Francisco F.C.").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nelson_Barahona&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Barahona was part of the [Panama U-20](/wiki/Panama_national_under-20_football_team "Panama national under-20 football team") squad that participated in the [2007 FIFA U-20 World Cup](/wiki/2007_FIFA_U-20_World_Cup "2007 FIFA U-20 World Cup") in Canada. He scored Panama's only goal of the tournament against [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic_national_under-20_football_team "Czech Republic national under-20 football team").[[10]](#cite_note-FIFA-10)

He made his senior debut for [Panama](/wiki/Panama_national_football_team "Panama national football team") on 22 August 2007 against [Guatemala](/wiki/Guatemala_national_football_team "Guatemala national football team") and has, as of 1 May 2015, earned a total of 45 caps, scoring 4 goals.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He represented his country in 13 [FIFA World Cup qualification](/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup_qualification "FIFA World Cup qualification") matches[[10]](#cite_note-FIFA-10) and was part of the [national team](/wiki/Panama_national_football_team "Panama national football team") that won the [2009 UNCAF Nations Cup](/wiki/2009_UNCAF_Nations_Cup "2009 UNCAF Nations Cup") in [Honduras](/wiki/Honduras "Honduras").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In 2011, he was called up by [Julio Dely Valdés](/wiki/Julio_Dely_Vald%C3%A9s "Julio Dely Valdés") to play at the [2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup](/wiki/2011_CONCACAF_Gold_Cup "2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup").
